meta.title: Buffett / Graham / Lynch Stock Analysis Rules
meta.description: A combined rule set derived from the investment philosophies of Buffett, Graham, and Lynch. A stock must pass ALL rules before consideration.

phases.name: Business Quality Gate
phases.description: Qualitative screen — understand the business before touching the numbers.

rules.name: Two-minute explanation test
rules.question: Can you explain what this company does and how it makes money in under two minutes?
rules.pass_condition: Yes — clear, simple explanation possible
rules.fail_condition: No — business is too complex or unclear
rules.notes: Lynch's cocktail party test. If you cannot explain it simply, you do not understand it well enough to own it.

rules.name: Durable competitive moat
rules.question: Does the company have a durable competitive advantage?
rules.pass_condition: Yes — identifiable moat: brand, network effect, switching costs, cost advantage, or regulatory license
rules.fail_condition: No moat or moat is deteriorating
rules.notes: Must be one of the five moat types. 'They are good at what they do' is not a moat.

rules.name: Management integrity check
rules.question: Is management honest, candid about failures, and shareholder-aligned?
rules.pass_condition: Yes — candid shareholder letters, low dilution, reasonable compensation
rules.fail_condition: Signs of self-dealing, excessive options, or misleading communication
rules.notes: Read at least 5 years of CEO letters. Look for candor about failures, not just celebration of wins.

rules.name: Investor domain familiarity
rules.question: Is this business in an industry you can follow and understand over time?
rules.pass_condition: Yes — you have or can develop genuine knowledge of this sector
rules.fail_condition: No — outside your circle of competence with no clear path to understanding
rules.action_on_fail: REJECT or defer until sufficient knowledge is gained
rules.notes: An edge in understanding the business beats financial cleverness. Invest in what you know.

phases.name: Financial Health Screen
phases.description: Quantitative balance sheet and earnings quality checks — Graham's discipline.

rules.name: Current ratio minimum
rules.pass_condition: Current ratio >= 2.0
rules.fail_condition: Current ratio < 1.5
rules.notes: Graham's minimum for financial safety. Below 1.5 signals potential liquidity risk.

rules.name: Long-term debt ceiling
rules.pass_condition: Long-term debt <= 2x working capital
rules.fail_condition: Long-term debt > 2x working capital
rules.notes: High debt amplifies every business mistake. A company shouldn't be drowning in debt relative to what it controls.

rules.name: Unbroken earnings record
rules.formula: Net Income / Shares Outstanding — check each year
rules.threshold: Positive every year for 10 consecutive years
rules.pass_condition: No annual loss in 10-year period
rules.fail_condition: Any year of negative earnings in 10-year period
rules.notes: Stability proves the moat is real, not cyclical luck. Source: company 10-K filings or Macrotrends.

rules.name: Return on equity — sustained
rules.threshold: >= 15% for each of the past 5 years
rules.pass_condition: ROE >= 15% consistently — not just average
rules.fail_condition: ROE < 15% in any of the past 5 years
rules.notes: Buffett's favorite single metric. A one-time spike does not count. Each year must clear the bar.

rules.name: Owner earnings positive and growing
rules.pass_condition: Owner earnings positive and trending upward over 5 years
rules.fail_condition: Negative owner earnings or declining trend
rules.notes: This is actual cash generated for owners — more honest than reported net income.

phases.name: Growth and Story Check
phases.description: Is the growth real, understandable, and not yet priced in?

rules.name: PEG ratio target
rules.formula: P/E Ratio / Annual EPS Growth Rate
rules.fail_condition: PEG > 2.0
rules.notes: Lynch's signature metric. PEG < 1 means you may be getting growth cheaply. PEG > 2 means growth is heavily priced in.

rules.name: Stock category classification
rules.question: Which of Lynch's six categories does this stock belong to?
categories: Slow Grower — large, mature, modest growth; value the dividend
Stalwart — large, steady 10-15% growers; sell at 30-50% gain
Fast Grower — small, aggressive 20-25% growers; highest reward and risk
Cyclical — tied to economic cycles; timing of entry is critical
Turnaround — troubled company with recovery potential; highest risk
Asset Play — hidden assets not reflected in stock price
rules.pass_condition: Category clearly identified and investment thesis matches that category's characteristics
rules.fail_condition: Cannot categorize or thesis does not match category
rules.notes: Each category requires a different analysis approach and exit strategy.

rules.name: Remaining opportunity size
rules.question: Is there still significant room for the company to grow — is the niche still being filled?
rules.pass_condition: Company has < 40% of its total addressable market
rules.fail_condition: Company dominates market with little room for expansion
rules.action_on_fail: Reassess — most easy gains may already be captured
rules.notes: Lynch: when a fast grower already saturates its market, the growth story is over. Find it early.

rules.name: High-return reinvestment ability
rules.formula: Net Operating Profit After Tax / (Total Equity + Total Debt - Cash)
rules.pass_condition: ROIC >= 15% and business has opportunities to reinvest at similar rates
rules.fail_condition: ROIC < 10% or no clear reinvestment runway
rules.notes: The best compounders earn well AND can deploy capital back into the business at high rates. This is the engine of long-term wealth.

phases.name: Valuation and Margin of Safety
phases.description: Never overpay — even for a great business. Price determines your return.

rules.name: P/E ratio anchor
rules.warning_condition: P/E between 15 and 20 — requires exceptional quality justification
rules.fail_condition: P/E > 25 without extraordinary circumstances
rules.action_on_fail: REJECT or wait for better entry price
rules.notes: Graham's combined rule: P/E × P/Book should not exceed 22.5. Can be relaxed slightly for high-quality compounders, but use as a starting anchor.

rules.name: Price-to-book ratio check
rules.formula: Market Capitalization / Book Value of Equity
rules.pass_condition: P/Book <= 1.5
rules.warning_condition: P/Book between 1.5 and 3.0 — only acceptable for very high ROE
rules.fail_condition: P/Book > 3.0 for average businesses
rules.action_on_fail: REJECT or require high ROE justification
rules.notes: Combined with VMS-01: P/E × P/Book <= 22.5 is Graham's combined screen.

rules.name: Discounted cash flow intrinsic value
rules.formula: Sum of (Owner Earnings Year N / (1 + discount_rate)^N) for N = 1 to 10, plus terminal value
rules.threshold: Market cap must be below calculated intrinsic value
rules.pass_condition: Current market cap < DCF intrinsic value estimate
rules.fail_condition: Current market cap >= DCF intrinsic value estimate
rules.action_on_fail: REJECT or wait for price to fall
rules.notes: Buffett: it is better to be approximately right than precisely wrong. Use conservative growth assumptions.

rules.name: Margin of safety minimum
rules.pass_condition: Current price is at least 25% below intrinsic value estimate
rules.fail_condition: Current price is within 15% of or above intrinsic value estimate
rules.notes: Graham's most important principle. The margin of safety cushions your analytical errors and market volatility. The bigger the better.

rules.name: Projected annual return gate
rules.pass_condition: Expected 10-year annualized return >= 10%
rules.fail_condition: Expected return < 8% annualized
rules.action_on_fail: REJECT — insufficient expected return for the risk taken
rules.notes: Buffett: if you wouldn't be happy holding for 10 years, don't hold for 10 minutes. Entry price is everything.

phases.name: Final Buy Decision and Position Management
phases.description: Pulling the trigger intelligently — sizing, documentation, and exit discipline.

rules.name: All prior phases passed with no unsure answers
rules.question: Did all rules in phases 1-4 receive a clear PASS — not 'probably' or 'mostly'?
rules.pass_condition: Yes — every rule has a definitive pass
rules.fail_condition: Any rule returned a warning, unsure, or partial pass
rules.action_on_fail: REJECT — do not invest on partial conviction
rules.notes: The system is only as strong as your willingness to walk away. Pretty good on a quality gate is a no.

rules.name: Written investment thesis required
rules.question: Have you written a concise thesis (3-5 sentences) explaining WHY you are buying this stock?
rules.pass_condition: Yes — thesis written and includes: what company does, why moat is durable, why price is attractive, and what would change your mind
rules.fail_condition: No written thesis, or thesis is vague
rules.action_on_fail: Do not buy until thesis is written
rules.notes: Lynch: if you can't explain it in a few sentences, you don't know why you're buying. The thesis also defines when to sell.

rules.name: Position size limit
rules.question: Is your planned position within 5-10% of total portfolio value?
rules.pass_condition: Planned position is within 5-10% of total portfolio value
rules.fail_condition: Planned position exceeds 10% of portfolio
rules.notes: Conviction is not the same as certainty. Even Buffett's highest-conviction bets were bounded by position size discipline.

rules.name: Pre-defined sell criteria
rules.question: Before buying, have you written down the specific conditions under which you will sell?
rules.pass_condition: Sell criteria documented and includes: thesis break triggers, extreme valuation exit, and rebalancing rules
rules.fail_condition: No pre-defined sell criteria
rules.action_on_fail: Do not buy until sell criteria are documented
rules.notes: Sell when the thesis breaks — not when the price drops. Decide this before emotions are involved.
